name: Olist Brazilian Ecommerce Data Product
description: The Olist Ecommerce data product provides a comprehensive view of ~100k orders placed on a Brazilian online marketplace between 2016 and 2018. It covers the full order lifecycle: customer placement, payment, seller fulfillment, carrier delivery, and post-delivery review. USE this data product to: analyze order volumes and revenue trends over time; compare seller performance by delivery speed, order volume, and review scores; evaluate payment method distribution and average order values; identify geographic patterns in customer demand and seller locations; measure delivery performance (on-time vs late); analyze product category popularity and pricing; assess customer satisfaction through review scores and comment sentiment; benchmark freight costs across regions and product types. DO NOT USE this data product for: real-time inventory or stock level analysis; individual customer identity resolution (IDs are anonymized); profit or margin analysis (cost data is not included, only prices and freight); marketing attribution or channel analysis; data beyond the 2016-2018 time window. Data covers: a single Brazilian marketplace (Olist). All monetary values are in BRL (Brazilian Real). Net revenue can be approximated by excluding orders with order_status = 'canceled' from revenue calculations.

tables.description: Central fact table for the ecommerce dataset. Each row represents one customer order. Grain: one row per order_id. Contains order status and key lifecycle timestamps from purchase through delivery. This is the hub table that connects to all other tables. RELATIONSHIPS: Each order belongs to one customer (many-to-one). An order can have multiple items in order_items, multiple payment rows in payments, and typically one review in reviews. JOIN GUIDANCE: Join to order_items ON order_id for item-level details (price, freight, product, seller). Join to payments ON order_id for payment method and amounts. Join to reviews ON order_id for customer review scores and comments. Join to customers ON customer_id for customer location. USE FOR: order-level KPIs (count, status distribution, delivery timeliness), time-series analysis of order volume, delivery performance metrics (actual vs estimated delivery date). DO NOT USE FOR: item-level or product-level analysis — use order_items joined to products instead.

columns.description: Unique identifier for the order. Primary key. Shared join key across order_items, payments, and reviews tables.

Identifier for the customer who placed this order. Foreign key to customers table ON customer_id (many-to-one: each order belongs to one customer). Note: this is an order-scoped ID; use customer_unique_id in the customers table for cross-order customer identification.

Current status of the order. Values include: 'delivered', 'shipped', 'canceled', 'unavailable', 'invoiced', 'processing', 'created', 'approved'. Most orders are 'delivered'. Filter to 'delivered' for completed-order analysis. Filter WHERE order_status != 'canceled' for net revenue calculations.
Timestamp when the customer placed the order. Primary time dimension for trend analysis. Format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS. Use CAST(order_purchase_timestamp AS TIMESTAMP) for date operations in SQL.
Timestamp when the order payment was approved. May be null for non-approved orders. Use to calculate payment processing time (approved_at minus purchase_timestamp).
Timestamp when the seller handed the order to the carrier. Use to calculate seller processing time (carrier_date minus approved_at).
Timestamp when the order was delivered to the customer. Compare to order_estimated_delivery_date to determine if delivery was on-time or late. May be null if not yet delivered.
Estimated delivery date shown to the customer at purchase time. Compare to order_delivered_customer_date to measure delivery accuracy. Orders delivered after this date are considered late.

tables.description: Item-level fact table. Each row represents one item within an order. Grain: one row per order_id + order_item_id (sequence number). Contains price and freight for each item, plus links to the product and seller. RELATIONSHIPS: Each order item belongs to one order (many-to-one via order_id). Each order item references one product (many-to-one via product_id). Each order item is fulfilled by one seller (many-to-one via seller_id). A product can appear in many order items. A seller can fulfill many items. JOIN GUIDANCE: Join to orders ON order_id for order timestamps and status. Join to products ON product_id for product category and attributes. Join to sellers ON seller_id for seller location. USE FOR: revenue analysis (price + freight_value), product-level and seller-level performance, item count per order, freight cost analysis. Net revenue (excluding cancellations) can be calculated by joining to orders and filtering WHERE order_status != 'canceled'. DO NOT USE FOR: payment method analysis — use payments table instead. Note: an order can have multiple items, possibly from different sellers.

columns.description: Identifier of the order this item belongs to. Foreign key to orders table ON order_id (many-to-one: each item belongs to one order, an order can have multiple items).
Sequential number identifying the item within the order (1, 2, 3...). Combined with order_id forms the grain of this table.
Identifier of the product purchased. Foreign key to products table ON product_id (many-to-one: each item references one product, a product can appear in many items).

Identifier of the seller who fulfilled this item. Foreign key to sellers table ON seller_id (many-to-one: each item is fulfilled by one seller, a seller can fulfill many items). Different items in the same order may come from different sellers.
Deadline by which the seller must hand the item to the carrier. Used to evaluate seller shipping compliance.
Price of the item in BRL (Brazilian Real). This is the item sale price, not including freight. Sum across items in an order for total order product value.
Freight/shipping cost for this item in BRL. Each item has its own freight value even within the same order. Sum price + freight_value for total item cost to customer.

tables.description: Product dimension table. Each row represents one unique product listed on the marketplace. Grain: one row per product_id. Contains product category and physical attributes. RELATIONSHIPS: A product can appear in many order items (one-to-many). JOIN GUIDANCE: Join to order_items ON product_id to connect products to orders. USE FOR: category-level revenue and volume analysis, product attribute analysis (size, weight, photo count), identifying top-selling categories. DO NOT USE FOR: inventory or stock level analysis (not available). Note: product_category_name is in Portuguese.

columns.description: Unique identifier for the product. Primary key. Join target from order_items ON product_id.

Product category name in Portuguese (e.g., 'beleza_saude' = health & beauty, 'informatica_acessorios' = computer accessories, 'cama_mesa_banho' = bed/table/bath). May contain nulls for uncategorized products.
Character length of the product name. Note: column name has a typo ('lenght' instead of 'length'). May be null.
Character length of the product description. Note: column name has a typo ('lenght' instead of 'length'). May be null.
Number of product photos in the listing. May be null.
Product weight in grams. Useful for freight cost analysis. May be null.
Product package length in centimeters. May be null.
Product package height in centimeters. May be null.
Product package width in centimeters. May be null.

tables.description: Customer dimension table. Each row represents a customer as seen in a specific order. Grain: one row per customer_id. Contains customer geographic information. IMPORTANT: customer_id is order-scoped (one per order). Use customer_unique_id to count distinct real customers across multiple orders. RELATIONSHIPS: Each customer can have multiple orders (one-to-many via customer_id in orders table). JOIN GUIDANCE: Join to orders ON customer_id to connect customers to their orders. USE FOR: geographic analysis of customer base (city, state, zip), counting unique customers (use customer_unique_id), regional demand patterns. DO NOT USE FOR: customer demographic or behavioral segmentation (no age, gender, or preference data available).

columns.description: Order-scoped customer identifier. Primary key. Join target from orders ON customer_id. WARNING: this ID is unique per order, not per real customer. Use customer_unique_id to deduplicate.
True unique customer identifier across all orders. Use this column (not customer_id) when counting distinct customers or analyzing repeat purchase behavior.
First 5 digits of the customer's zip code. Can be used for geographic clustering.
City where the customer is located. All lowercase. Largest concentration is 'sao paulo'.
Two-letter Brazilian state code (e.g., 'SP' = São Paulo, 'RJ' = Rio de Janeiro, 'MG' = Minas Gerais). Use for state-level geographic analysis.

tables.description: Seller dimension table. Each row represents one unique seller on the marketplace. Grain: one row per seller_id. Contains seller geographic information. RELATIONSHIPS: Each seller can fulfill many order items (one-to-many via seller_id in order_items table). JOIN GUIDANCE: Join to order_items ON seller_id to connect sellers to the items they fulfilled. USE FOR: seller location analysis, geographic seller-customer distance analysis, seller concentration by city/state. DO NOT USE FOR: seller performance metrics — join order_items to orders and reviews for that.

columns.description: Unique identifier for the seller. Primary key. Join target from order_items ON seller_id.
First 5 digits of the seller's zip code.
City where the seller is located. All lowercase.
Two-letter Brazilian state code where the seller is located.

tables.description: Payment fact table. Each row represents one payment installment for an order. Grain: one row per order_id + payment_sequential. An order can have multiple payment rows if paid in installments or with multiple methods. RELATIONSHIPS: Each payment belongs to one order (many-to-one via order_id). An order can have multiple payment rows. JOIN GUIDANCE: Join to orders ON order_id for order context. USE FOR: payment method analysis, installment behavior, total payment value per order (SUM payment_value GROUP BY order_id), revenue by payment type. DO NOT USE FOR: item-level revenue attribution — use order_items.price for that.

columns.description: Identifier of the order this payment belongs to. Foreign key to orders table ON order_id (many-to-one: each payment belongs to one order, an order can have multiple payment rows).
Sequential number for payments within the same order (1, 2, 3...). If an order has multiple payment methods or installments, each gets a separate row.
Payment method used. Values: 'credit_card', 'boleto' (Brazilian bank slip), 'debit_card', 'voucher', 'not_defined'. Credit card is the most common.
Number of installments chosen by the customer for this payment. Common in Brazil — customers frequently split payments into 2-12 monthly installments.
Value of this payment in BRL. SUM(payment_value) grouped by order_id gives total order payment. May not exactly match SUM(price + freight_value) from order_items due to vouchers or rounding.

tables.description: Review fact table. Each row represents one customer review for an order. Grain: one row per review_id (typically one review per order, but some orders may have multiple). Contains review score and optional text feedback. RELATIONSHIPS: Each review references one order (many-to-one via order_id). Most orders have one review. JOIN GUIDANCE: Join to orders ON order_id for order context and timestamps. USE FOR: customer satisfaction analysis, average review scores by category/seller/region, identifying low-rated sellers or products, review response time analysis. DO NOT USE FOR: real-time sentiment monitoring. Note: review comments are in Portuguese and many are null.

columns.description: Unique identifier for the review. Primary key.

Identifier of the order being reviewed. Foreign key to orders table ON order_id (many-to-one: each review references one order, most orders have one review). Use to join reviews to order/product/seller data.
Customer satisfaction score from 1 (worst) to 5 (best). Average across the dataset is ~4.0. Use for satisfaction KPIs and comparisons.
Title/subject of the review comment. In Portuguese. Often null — many customers only leave a score without text.
Body text of the review comment. In Portuguese. Often null. When present, contains detailed customer feedback about the product or delivery experience.
Date when the customer submitted the review. Format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.
Timestamp when the review was answered/acknowledged. Format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.

defaults.description: If description is missing, use the field name as description.
